target.title: fix #95495: [Bug]: 2026.6.9 silently relocates memory store with no migration, forcing a full re-embed (1499 files) with zero upgrade-time warning
target.body:
## Summary

- Migrates legacy Memory Core sidecar indexes from `$OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR/memory/<agent>.sqlite` into the per-agent SQLite database through a doctor state migration.
- Keeps normal memory runtime startup from reading old sidecar databases, so clearing or rebuilding the canonical per-agent index does not resurrect stale memory state.
- Adds upgrade regression tests for the empty-canonical startup case, explicit doctor migration import, already-populated canonical database preservation, and sqlite-vec vector-backed search after migration.
- Restores legacy `chunks_vec` semantic vector rows into `memory_index_chunks_vec` before archiving handled sidecars.

## Origin / follow-up

N/A — this is an update to the existing issue fix PR, not a sibling follow-up to a merged change.

## Root Cause

- **Root cause:** OpenClaw 2026.6.9 moved the memory index store from `$OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR/memory/<agent>.sqlite` to `$OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR/agents/<agent>/agent/openclaw-agent.sqlite`, but the upgrade path only initialized the new per-agent schema and never imported the old sidecar rows.
- **Why this is root-cause fix:** The patch gives the legacy sidecar import one explicit persisted-state owner: Memory Core doctor state migration. Runtime startup now continues to open only the canonical per-agent database, while doctor migration performs the one-time sidecar-to-agent import and archives the old sidecar so future startup cannot read stale state.
- **Why it matters / User impact:** Users upgrading from 2026.6.8-era memory indexes can preserve indexed memory state instead of rebuilding embeddings for large memory trees, while users who deliberately clear or rebuild the canonical index are protected from old sidecar rows reappearing during ordinary startup.
- **Architecture / source-of-truth check:** Memory Core doctor state migrations own the migration contract for legacy persisted state; `ensureMemoryIndexSchema()` owns only the canonical schema for the database it is given.
- **What did NOT change:** This PR only touches Memory Core memory-index migration and the host SDK helper needed by that migration. It does not change provider routing, embedding generation, memory search ranking, session transcript storage, or normal runtime database selection.
- **Fix classification:** Root-cause persisted-state migration ownership fix.
- **Related PR scan:** `check-upstream-fixed` reported `CONTINUE_ALLOWED`; `origin/main` did not contain an equivalent fix for PR #95631 at the checked head.

## What Changed

- Removed the legacy sidecar path from runtime schema startup in `extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager-sync-ops.ts:790`.
- Exposed `importLegacyMemorySidecarIndex()` as an explicit schema helper in `packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/memory-schema.ts:320` and re-exported it through the host SDK subpaths.
- Added doctor state migration `memory-core-legacy-sidecar-index-to-agent-sqlite` in `extensions/memory-core/doctor-contract-api.ts:435`.
- Archives migrated sidecar files as `.migrated` after explicit doctor migration handling, including the already-populated canonical database case.
- Loads sqlite-vec during doctor migration and creates the canonical vector table from legacy dimensions before copying matching legacy `chunks_vec` rows.
- Added regression tests in `packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/memory-schema.test.ts:93` and `extensions/memory-core/doctor-contract-api.test.ts:400`, including vector-backed search after migration.
- Revalidated the plugin SDK surface report after rebasing onto current main; the PR now accounts for the one public/callable SDK export added for explicit sidecar import.

## Review findings addressed

- **RF-001:** Added focused regression coverage with sqlite-vec enabled and non-empty vector-backed search after doctor migration; the test imports legacy `chunks_vec`, restores `memory_index_chunks_vec`, and verifies `searchVector()` returns `chunk-1`.
- **RF-002:** A redacted real upgrade or doctor plus memory status/search terminal proof was not run in this environment; the proof boundary below explicitly states that only local source-level upgrade-state proof with real SQLite and sqlite-vec search was completed.
- **RF-003:** Same as RF-002: the requested real `openclaw update` or user-visible doctor/status/search proof is still unavailable here, and the unavailable environment/context plus completed local verification are disclosed below.
- **RF-004:** Fixed the archive risk by restoring matching legacy `chunks_vec` rows into canonical `memory_index_chunks_vec` before the sidecar archive step.
- **RF-005:** The new public plugin SDK callable remains intentionally limited to the explicit doctor migration path; maintainer acceptance of that SDK boundary is still called out as a review decision.
- **RF-006:** The contributor proof remains local test output against temporary SQLite databases rather than a real user-visible upgrade/doctor/status/search run; this limitation is disclosed under `What was not tested` and `Unavailable environment/context`.
- **RF-007:** Manual review is still needed for the external proof boundary and SDK boundary decision; the previously reported vector-row migration defect is addressed in this update.
- **RF-008:** Restored vector rows before archiving sidecars by loading sqlite-vec in the doctor migration, creating the canonical vector table from legacy dimensions, and copying matching `chunks_vec` rows during explicit sidecar import.

## Regression Test Plan

- **Target test file:** `packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/memory-schema.test.ts` and `extensions/memory-core/doctor-contract-api.test.ts`.
- **Scenario locked in:** A legacy sidecar exists beside an empty canonical per-agent database; schema startup must leave canonical rows empty. A doctor migration run against a valid legacy sidecar must import rows into the per-agent database and archive the sidecar. A doctor migration run with legacy `chunks_vec` rows must restore the canonical sqlite-vec table and return the migrated chunk through vector-backed search. A doctor migration run when canonical rows already exist must preserve canonical rows and archive the old sidecar.
- **Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail:** The tests use real `node:sqlite` databases, sqlite-vec loading, and real schema/migration/search code paths, so they catch the original missing-import upgrade path, the stale-sidecar startup path, and the vector-row archive risk without mocking the storage layer.

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/memory-schema.test.ts extensions/memory-core/doctor-contract-api.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager-search.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager-db.test.ts src/commands/doctor-config-preflight.state-migration.test.ts test/scripts/plugin-sdk-surface-report.test.ts
node scripts/plugin-sdk-surface-report.mjs --check
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor/shared/missing-configured-plugin-install.test.ts
pnpm exec oxfmt --check scripts/plugin-sdk-surface-report.mjs test/scripts/plugin-sdk-surface-report.test.ts extensions/memory-core/doctor-contract-api.ts extensions/memory-core/doctor-contract-api.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager-sync-ops.ts packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/memory-schema.ts packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/memory-schema.test.ts packages/memory-host-sdk/src/engine-storage.ts src/plugin-sdk/memory-core-host-engine-storage.ts && git diff --check
pnpm tsgo:core
pnpm tsgo:core:test
```

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Upgrading from the legacy memory sidecar store can preserve existing memory index rows through an explicit doctor state migration, while normal runtime startup no longer resurrects stale sidecar state when the canonical per-agent database is empty.
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw PR worktree at current head, using real `node:sqlite` databases in temporary state directories through the Memory Core schema, doctor migration, and manager test paths.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```bash
cd /media/vdb/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/pr-95631
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/memory-schema.test.ts extensions/memory-core/doctor-contract-api.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager-search.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager-db.test.ts src/commands/doctor-config-preflight.state-migration.test.ts test/scripts/plugin-sdk-surface-report.test.ts
node scripts/plugin-sdk-surface-report.mjs --check
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor/shared/missing-configured-plugin-install.test.ts
pnpm exec oxfmt --check scripts/plugin-sdk-surface-report.mjs test/scripts/plugin-sdk-surface-report.test.ts extensions/memory-core/doctor-contract-api.ts extensions/memory-core/doctor-contract-api.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager-sync-ops.ts packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/memory-schema.ts packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/memory-schema.test.ts packages/memory-host-sdk/src/engine-storage.ts src/plugin-sdk/memory-core-host-engine-storage.ts && git diff --check
pnpm tsgo:core
pnpm tsgo:core:test
```

- **Evidence after fix:**

```text
[test] passed 4 Vitest shards in 225.86s
Unit-fast shard: 1 test file passed, 5 tests passed
Unit shard: 1 test file passed, 7 tests passed
Commands shard: 1 test file passed, 6 tests passed
Extension-memory shard: 3 test files passed, 33 tests passed

node scripts/plugin-sdk-surface-report.mjs --check
public package SDK entrypoints:
  entrypoints: 321
  exports: 10328
  callable exports: 5185

node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor/shared/missing-configured-plugin-install.test.ts
Test Files 1 passed (1)
Tests 71 passed (71)

Checking formatting...
All matched files use the correct format.

pnpm tsgo:core exited 0
pnpm tsgo:core:test exited 0
```

- **Observed result after fix:** The doctor migration imports legacy sidecar rows into the per-agent SQLite database, restores migrated `chunks_vec` rows into `memory_index_chunks_vec`, returns the migrated chunk through vector-backed search, and archives the sidecar only after successful import; if the per-agent index already has rows, doctor migration preserves canonical rows and archives the stale sidecar; normal schema startup leaves an empty canonical database empty even when an old sidecar exists.
- **What was not tested:** I did not run a packaged release-beta upgrade install, redacted real `openclaw update` or doctor plus memory status/search terminal proof, or any external embedding provider. The proof here is local source-level upgrade-state proof with real SQLite files and sqlite-vec search, not third-party live proof.
- **Unavailable environment/context:** No external provider account, STS path, packaged beta install, or remote customer state directory was used in this verification; the completed verification is local upgrade-state proof against real SQLite state files.

## Merge risk

- **Risk labels considered:** auth-provider, automation, compatibility, security-boundary, session-state.
- **Risk explanation:** The auth-provider and security-boundary signals come from copying existing embedding cache columns, not from changing provider authentication or request signing. The compatibility and session-state risks are the intended migration surface: legacy memory index state moves into the canonical per-agent SQLite database. The automation risk is limited to adding a doctor state migration entry.
- **Why acceptable:** The import only runs through the explicit doctor migration path, validates the legacy sidecar schema shape before copying rows, restores matching legacy vector rows into the canonical sqlite-vec table before archive, skips overwrite when canonical index rows already exist, and archives handled sidecar files to prevent later stale startup reads. Normal runtime startup no longer opens the legacy sidecar.
- **Patch quality notes:** Added broad catches are bounded to migration discovery/per-source reporting so one unreadable legacy source does not abort the whole doctor migration; the schema import catch rolls back a savepoint and rethrows. Vector restoration is bounded to explicit sidecar import and does not change normal runtime startup. The `return null` path is the existing doctor contract for no detected legacy state. The larger diff is mostly SQLite fixture and regression coverage needed to prove the migration ownership boundary.
- **Maintainer-ready confidence:** 88/100; the remaining evidence boundary is that this was verified locally with real SQLite state files rather than a packaged release-beta upgrade install or external provider run.
target.author: mushuiyu886
